article_meta.title: Four days into war with Iran, at least one of the United States’ Gulf allies is already running low on crucial intercept

reasoning: The model avoided most direct quote attributions but relied on non-standard bias labels, included at least one partially attributed phrase among examples, raised some questionable bias claims, and missed concrete biased framing in the author’s own prose.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Mostly discussed structural omissions, but one example cited a phrase that is attributed/quoted ("burning through...") among its evidence.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Claims about placement/confirmation bias are speculative; Trump’s prominence as current president makes emphasis non-biased news value; article also contains countervailing views, weakening ‘confirmation bias’.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-voice framing like "it’s a numbers game," "US rivals like China will be watching closely," and "The immediate concern though... not the US," and "daunting math."
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All four labels (Selection Bias, Framing Bias, Placement Bias, Confirmation Bias) are outside the allowed taxonomy; closer matches would be Bias by Omission, Slant, or Bias by Story Choice and Placement.
